summary: chai-check-error typosquats chai's check-error and executes remote code on import
details: On require/import, index.js calls resolveConfig() at top level which performs an HTTPS GET to a base64-obfuscated host (jsonkeeper.com/b/JOCBY), parses the response as JSON, and executes the `.cookie` field as code via `new Function('require',...)(require)` — granting arbitrary remote code execution to whoever controls the paste. jsonkeeper.com is a public anonymous paste service whose contents are mutable, so the executed payload can be changed by the attacker at any time without republishing the package. The package name `chai-check-error` mimics the legitimate chaijs `check-error` utility: it copies the author/contributor metadata, repository URL, and re-exports the exact public API (compatibleInstance, compatibleConstructor, compatibleMessage, getMessage, getConstructorName) so a developer who mistypes the dependency name receives a working API plus the import-time RCE side effect.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
